job.scriptExecutionDesc: Quickly execute Shell or Python scripts on target hosts, supporting selection from script library or temporary input
job.fileDistributionDesc: Batch distribute files or directories to target hosts, supporting overwrite policies and concurrency control
job.scheduledTaskDesc: Create and manage periodically executed jobs, supporting Cron expressions and custom execution strategies
job.operationsOverviewDesc: Review job execution trends and success rate changes over the last 7 or 30 days.
job.executionTrendDesc: Track daily execution, success, and failure counts.
job.successRateOverviewDesc: Current period success rate compared with the previous period of the same length.
job.quickExecDesc: Quickly execute scripts or Playbooks on target hosts. Supports selecting existing scripts from job templates, or entering script content for direct execution.
job.jobNamePlaceholder: Enter job name, e.g.: Clean disk space
job.execParamsPlaceholder: Parameters passed when executing the script, same format as terminal execution, e.g.: ./test.sh xxxx xxx xxx
job.timeoutHint: Script execution timeout. Will be automatically terminated when exceeded.
job.searchHostPlaceholder: Search host name or IP, supports fuzzy matching
job.dangerousCommandDesc: Configure dangerous command rules. When a user executes a matching command, it will be intercepted or require secondary confirmation based on the handling strategy
job.dangerousPathDesc: Configure dangerous path rules. When a user operates on a matching path, it will be intercepted or require secondary confirmation based on the handling strategy
job.ruleNamePlaceholder: Enter rule name, e.g.: Prohibit forced recursive deletion
job.matchPatternHelpCommand: Supports plain string matching and regular expressions. Use regex for more precise matching.
job.matchPatternHelpPath: Supports plain path matching and regular expressions. Use regex for more precise path matching.
job.matchPatternHelpPathExact: Exact match: triggers only when the input path exactly matches the target path.
job.matchPatternHelpPathRegex: Regular expression: use regex syntax for pattern matching. Escape special characters when needed (e.g. . -> \.).
job.matchPatternExample3: DROP\s+(TABLE|DATABASE) - Match DROP TABLE or DATABASE
job.handleStrategyHelp:
Forbidden: Directly intercept the command, execution not allowed;
Confirmation Required: Prompt user to confirm before execution.
job.handleStrategyHelpCommand:
Forbidden: Directly intercept the command, execution not allowed;
Confirmation Required: Prompt user to confirm before execution.
job.handleStrategyHelpPath:
Forbid Distribution: Directly intercept the path, distribution not allowed;
Confirmation Required: Prompt user to confirm before distribution.
job.ruleNamePathPlaceholder: Enter rule name, e.g.: System core directory protection
job.matchPatternPathExample4: /tmp/.*\.(sh|bin) - Regex match executables in temp directory
job.deleteRuleConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this rule? This action cannot be undone.
job.descriptionPlaceholder: Briefly describe the purpose of the rule
job.targetManagementDesc: Manage job execution targets. Each target is configured with an execution driver, and the system automatically selects the execution channel based on the target's driver during job execution.
job.deleteTargetConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this target? This action cannot be undone.
job.driverHelp: The system will automatically select the execution channel based on the target's driver during job execution
job.windowsWinrmHelp: Windows targets use WinRM. The default port is 5986 for HTTPS and 5985 for HTTP.
job.windowsCredentialHelp: Please select a WinRM credential for the Windows target.
job.windowsTestConnectionNote: Test connection note: Windows targets are validated using the current WinRM settings, and the result only reflects the current configuration.
job.testConnectionNote: Test connection note: The test result only indicates that the current configuration is valid. Actual execution is based on the job runtime.
job.scriptLibraryDesc: Manage reusable script resources, supporting Shell, Python, Bat, PowerShell and more
job.playbookLibraryDesc: Playbooks are collections of Ansible automation scripts for batch execution of operations tasks. You can upload, manage, and version control Playbooks here, and assign them to different organizations.
job.creator: Creator
job.deleteScriptConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this script? This action cannot be undone.
job.paramNameHint: Used in Jinja2 templates, e.g. {{ param_name }}
job.paramOptionsPlaceholder: Enter option and press Enter to add
job.dragUploadText: Click or drag ZIP file here to upload
job.dragUploadHint: Upload a compressed Playbook directory containing playbook.yml and README.md
job.deletePlaybookConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this playbook? This action cannot be undone.
job.fileDistDesc: Quickly distribute files to target hosts. Supports uploading files or archives, with configurable target paths and overwrite strategies.
job.fileUploadDrag: Click or drag files here to upload
job.fileUploadHint: Supports files or archives, max 500MB per file
job.fileDistTargetPathHint: Files will be distributed to this path on target hosts
job.fileDistTimeoutHint: File distribution timeout. Will be automatically terminated when exceeded.
job.dangerousPathCheckFailed: Unable to check dangerous path rules. Continuing execution.
job.createTaskDesc: Create a new scheduled task, configure execution strategy and content. Supports simple strategy for quick configuration, or use advanced strategy to customize Cron expressions.
job.deleteTaskConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this scheduled task? This action cannot be undone.
job.skipIfRunning: Skip if previous run not finished
job.queueWait: Queue and wait for previous run
job.jobRecordDesc: View execution history of all jobs, including manual execution, scheduled tasks, and API-triggered jobs. Supports filtering by status, type, and time range.
job.editTaskDesc: Edit the execution strategy and content of the scheduled task.
job.scriptTemplateBat:
@echo off
REM Write your batch script here
job.scriptTemplatePython:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Write your Python script here
job.upgradeWarning: Uploading a new version will overwrite the current version. We recommend downloading the current version as a backup first.
job.selectNewZip: Click to select a new ZIP file
job.newVersionHint: Leave empty to auto-increment by +0.0.1 from current version
job.nodeManagerPlaybookNotSupported: Hosts from Node Manager do not support Playbook execution
job.forbiddenCommandMessage: The script contains the following forbidden commands and cannot be executed:
job.confirmCommandMessage: The script contains the following commands that require confirmation:
job.confirmExecuteQuestion: Do you want to proceed with execution?
job.fileDistributionNotSupported: Re-execution is not supported for file distribution jobs
job.forbiddenPathMessage: The target path contains the following forbidden paths and cannot be executed:
job.confirmPathMessage: The target path contains the following paths that require confirmation:
